raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on surface-level writing polish, substantive argument and claim calibration, or novelty/positioning against prior work?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section contains thesis, object-of-evaluation, and contribution framing that may need reframing under a broad help request.","R1.2":"The argument links user-intent alignment, majority-vote labels, accuracy, and alignment conclusions, so consistency checking is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The reported benchmark and accuracy numbers are used to support alignment claims, making evidence-claim alignment a central responsibility.","R1.4":"The section makes a first-of-its-kind GNN novelty claim while citing closely related GNN work, so novelty assessment is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"A generic request to help with a paper section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The artifact contains potentially strong claims about user intent alignment and novelty, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within projection rather than editing or evaluating the artifact directly.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported methodological conclusions.","RX.5":"Responsibilities should remain traceable to features of the delegation and artifact, such as the alignment and novelty claims."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on surface-level writing polish, substantive argument and claim calibration, or novelty/positioning against prior work?

rationale.R1.1: The section contains thesis, object-of-evaluation, and contribution framing that may need reframing under a broad help request.
rationale.R1.2: The argument links user-intent alignment, majority-vote labels, accuracy, and alignment conclusions, so consistency checking is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The reported benchmark and accuracy numbers are used to support alignment claims, making evidence-claim alignment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The section makes a first-of-its-kind GNN novelty claim while citing closely related GNN work, so novelty assessment is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request to help with a paper section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains potentially strong claims about user intent alignment and novelty, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection rather than editing or evaluating the artifact directly.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported methodological conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibilities should remain traceable to features of the delegation and artifact, such as the alignment and novelty claims.
